targets.boundary: Global operations under Microsoft’s carbon inventory
targets.notes: Microsoft plans to achieve this goal by reducing Scope 3 emissions by more than half and reducing Scope 1 and 2 market-based emissions to near zero by the middle of the decade through energy efficiency and reaching 100% renewable energy by 2025.
targets.title: Carbon negative by 2030

targets.boundary: All carbon the company has emitted directly or through electricity consumption since 1975
targets.notes: By 2050 Microsoft will remove from the atmosphere an equivalent amount of all the carbon the company has emitted either directly or by its electricity consumption since being founded in 1975.
targets.title: Carbon removal of all historical emissions by 2050

targets.boundary: Global Scope 1, Scope 2 market-based, and Scope 3 business air travel emissions
targets.notes: Microsoft’s target is to be carbon neutral every year since fiscal year 2013 by matching emissions within the carbon neutrality boundary with an equivalent amount of carbon offsets; starting in FY23, Scope 3 business air travel emissions follow management’s criteria as reported under Category 6 – Business Travel with SAFc.
targets.title: Annual carbon neutrality from FY2013 onward

targets.boundary: All carbon the company has emitted directly or by electrical consumption since 1975
targets.notes: Per Microsoft’s carbon negative target, by 2050 it expects to have removed from the environment all the carbon the company has emitted either directly or by electrical consumption since it was founded in 1975.
targets.title: Remove all historical emissions by 2050 per carbon negative target
